personal.name: Sunpil Kim
personal.title: Postdoctoral Researcher, IBS Center for Cognition and Sociality
personal.summary: Postdoctoral researcher studying dopamine-induced signaling in the brain, visuosocial memory, and unsupervised behavior mapping. Ph.D. from KU-KIST Graduate School, Korea University (Thesis advisor: C. Justin Lee). Last update: 2026. 5. 5.

experience.company: IBS Center for Cognition and Sociality

highlights: Dissertation: Study of dopamine-induced signaling in the brain and visuosocial memory

items: Cultured cell imaging (Calcium, Fura-2, and various sensors)
Social behaviors (3-chamber test and five-trial social memory test)
General behaviors (Open field test, novel object recognition and novel place recognition test)
Fear-associated behavior (Passive avoidance test and contextual fear conditioning)
Patch clamping (Cultured cell and acute brain slice)
Stereotaxic surgery (guide cannulation, microdialysis, drug infusion, virus injection)
Homepage development (IBS behavior analysis center, https://subtle.ibs.re.kr/)

projects.name: SUBTLE Behavior Analysis Platform
projects.description: IBS behavior analysis center homepage and unsupervised behavior mapping framework using spectrogram-UMAP-based temporal link embedding.

sections.title: Awards
entries: Outstanding presentation award (2018 5th SGFP Annual Conference)
Best presentation award (2022 25th KSBNS Annual Meeting)

sections.title: Fellowships
entries: 2017–2021: Global Fellowship (NRF, South Korea) – ~17,000 USD/year
